title: wui_fire
description: A wildland–urban interface fire erupts at the edge of a suburb, threatening homes and displacing residents.

timeline.title: Fire Ignition at Edge
timeline.description: Smoke reported at the wildland–urban interface boundary. First units respond.

timeline.title: Structures Threatened
timeline.description: Fire has reached the first row of homes. Triage structure defense priorities.

timeline.title: Evacuation Orders
timeline.description: Mandatory evacuation ordered for 500 residences in affected zone.

timeline.title: Spotting Ahead of Fire
timeline.description: Embers landing on rooftops blocks ahead of main fire. Deploy spot suppression crews.

timeline.title: Utility Shutoff
timeline.description: Gas and power companies requested to shut off service to the threatened area.

timeline.title: Lines Holding
timeline.description: Firefighters hold the line at the second row of homes. Fire begins to diminish.

node-1.title: Fire at WUI Boundary
node-1.situation: A fire has ignited in open space adjacent to a residential neighborhood. The WUI boundary is not clearly marked and the first homes are only 200 meters from the flames. Wind is gusting.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Initiate evacuation of the adjacent neighborhood, request mutual aid, and begin structure triage.

options.label: B
options.text: Focus all resources on fire suppression and defer evacuation decisions to law enforcement.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for the fire to approach structures before committing to a response plan.

node-2.title: Structure Triage
node-2.situation: Dozens of homes are now threatened. Resources are insufficient to defend every structure. Some homes have defensible space while others are surrounded by heavy vegetation.
node-2.question: How do you allocate your limited crews?

options.label: A
options.text: Prioritize homes with good defensible space where crews can safely engage, and note others for later.

options.label: B
options.text: Defend all homes equally, spreading resources as thin as necessary.

node-3.title: Ember Cast Threat
node-3.situation: Firebrands are landing on rooftops and in yards several blocks ahead of the main fire front, igniting new spot fires in the neighborhood.
node-3.question: How do you address the ember threat?

options.label: A
options.text: Deploy a roving spot crew to extinguish ember ignitions ahead of the fire and request additional engines.

options.label: B
options.text: Rely on residents who have not evacuated to handle ember ignitions on their own property.

node-4.title: Utility Coordination
node-4.situation: A gas line ruptures near a burning structure, creating an additional hazard. The utility company needs incident command authorization to shut off service to the neighborhood grid.
node-4.question: What do you authorize?

options.label: A
options.text: Authorize immediate shutoff of gas and power to the affected grid and establish a safety perimeter.

options.label: B
options.text: Delay utility shutoff to avoid disrupting nearby households not directly threatened.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The fire has been held at the established line. All residents evacuated safely, utilities are shut off, and the neighborhood has been protected. Recovery operations begin.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
